Title: Arabidopsis Tubby domain-containing F-box proteins positively regulate immunity by modulating PI4Kβ protein levels

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tubby-like protein with N-terminal F-box domain and C-terminal Tubby domain; F-box protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes immunity by reducing PI4Kβ protein levels, thereby likely lowering PI4P homeostasis; TLP6 overexpression causes autoimmune dwarfism, enhanced salicylic acid accumulation, and enhanced resistance to Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced upon infection, especially after Ha Emwa1; transcriptional upregulation from 0.5 dpi to at least 6 dpi in resistant plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during oomycete infection; overexpression under CaMV 35S causes strong phenotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis; dominant-negative overexpression; co-immunoprecipitation; IP-MS; ubiquitination assay; proteasome inhibitor assay; epistasis analysis; pathogen infection assays; PI4Kβ2 protein immunoblot
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PI4Kβ1; PI4Kβ2; TLP2; TLP1; TLP10; ASK proteins
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis Noco2 or Emwa1; MG132; Carfilzomib
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS; microarray expression browser data; protein abundance reference from PaxDB
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions through SCF-type E3 ligase activity and requires the F-box domain for PI4Kβ2 destabilization and increased ubiquitination. TLP6 overexpression does not further enhance pi4kβ1,2 autoimmunity, supporting PI4Kβs as main downstream targets.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TLP6 overexpression enhances Arabidopsis immunity and causes autoimmune dwarfism. 2. TLP6 interacts with PI4Kβ proteins and reduces PI4Kβ2 abundance in an F-box- and proteasome-dependent manner. 3. TLP6 overexpression increases PI4Kβ2 ubiquitination and phenocopies pi4kβ1,2, linking TLP6 to phosphoinositide-mediated immune regulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes severe dwarfism, leaf curliness, short roots, root hair defects, and autoimmune growth penalties.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential immune-enhancing target, but constitutive overexpression causes strong growth penalties; deployment would require controlled or inducible expression rather than constitutive activation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tubby-like protein with N-terminal F-box domain and C-terminal Tubby domain; F-box protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with TLP6 to promote immunity; overexpression enhances resistance and dominant-negative TLP2 was used to capture PI4Kβ interactors
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced upon Ha Emwa1 infection; similar co-regulation to TLP6
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during infection; overexpression under CaMV 35S enhances immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in snc1; IP-MS using dominant-negative TLP2; co-immunoprecipitation; pathogen infection assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dominant-negative TLP2 lacking F-box domain enriched candidate substrates, especially PI4Kβ1 and PI4Kβ2, supporting redundant substrate recognition within the TLP clade.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TLP2 is a close TLP6 homolog that enhances immunity when overexpressed. 2. Dominant-negative TLP2 pulled down PI4Kβ1 and PI4Kβ2 in IP-MS, identifying them as candidate substrates. 3. TLP2 likely functions redundantly with TLP6 in SCF-mediated degradation of negative immune regulators.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in snc1 causes autoimmunity-associated dwarfism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential redundant immune regulator, but constitutive activation is associated with growth defects.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tubby-like protein with F-box and Tubby domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with TLP6/TLP2 as a positive immune regulator when overexpressed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in snc1; pathogen infection assay; higher-order knockout analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as one of the redundant TLPs; present in DN-TLP2 pull-down in one repeat. Quintuple tlp1,2,5,6,10 mutant did not show reduced immunity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TLP1 overexpression enhances immunity in snc1. 2. TLP1 is part of a redundant TLP subclade with TLP2/5/6/10. 3. Loss of the five-gene clade does not cause overt immune deficiency under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in snc1 causes dwarfism-associated autoimmunity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a standalone engineering target based on current evidence due to redundancy and likely growth tradeoffs.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tubby-like protein with F-box and Tubby domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with TLP6 as a positive regulator of immunity when overexpressed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in snc1; pathogen infection assay; quintuple knockout analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of redundant TLP clade; not specifically validated biochemically against PI4Kβs in this study.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TLP5 overexpression enhances immunity in snc1. 2. TLP5 belongs to the redundant TLP1/2/5/6/10 immune-promoting clade. 3. Higher-order loss including TLP5 does not reduce basal immunity under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Autoimmunity-associated growth defects upon overexpression in snc1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited immediate value alone due to redundancy and growth penalty risk.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tubby-like protein with F-box and Tubby domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with TLP6 as a positive immune regulator when overexpressed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in snc1; pathogen infection assay; IP-MS support for association with DN-TLP2; quintuple knockout analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected in DN-TLP2 pull-downs, supporting clade association and potential oligomerization/redundancy.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TLP10 overexpression enhances immunity in snc1. 2. TLP10 is part of the redundant TLP immune-promoting subclade. 3. Genetic removal within the quintuple mutant does not lower immunity under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Autoimmunity-associated dwarfism upon overexpression in snc1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited standalone value because of redundancy and growth penalties.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid involved in cellular organization and immune-related signaling/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss together with PI4Kβ2 enhances immunity and causes autoimmune dwarfism, elevated SA, short roots, and root hair defects
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low-abundance protein according to PaxDB reference
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant phenotyping; IP-MS; co-IP support for PI4Kβ family interaction with TLPs; pathogen assays; SA measurement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function was analyzed primarily through the redundant pi4kβ1,2 double mutant rather than PI4Kβ1 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PI4Kβ1 is a redundant phosphoinositide biosynthetic enzyme acting with PI4Kβ2. 2. Loss of PI4Kβ1 together with PI4Kβ2 causes enhanced immunity and developmental defects. 3. PI4Kβ1 was identified as a likely TLP substrate in IP-MS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout with PI4Kβ2 causes severe developmental defects including dwarfism and root abnormalities.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing PI4Kβ activity can enhance immunity but causes strong developmental penalties, limiting direct deployment.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plasma membrane and other organellar membranes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane phosphoinositide involved in cellular and immune-related homeostasis; precursor of PI(4,5)P2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TLP6 promotes PI4Kβ2 ubiquitination and proteasome-dependent degradation; loss together with PI4Kβ1 enhances immunity and developmental defects, while PI4Kβ2 overexpression complements pi4kβ1,2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low abundance protein according to PaxDB reference
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 2HA-PI4Kβ2 protein decreases after infection and upon TLP6 coexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; co-IP; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable transgenic complementation; immunoblotting; proteasome inhibitor assay; ubiquitination assay; genetic epistasis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ha Emwa1 and Ha Noco2 infection; MG132; Carfilzomib
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TLP6-dependent depletion of PI4Kβ2 requires the F-box domain and is blocked by MG132. High-MW PI4Kβ2 species accumulate with Carfilzomib, supporting polyubiquitination. PI4Kβ2 overexpression alone did not reduce immunity under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PI4Kβ2 is a direct TLP-associated phosphoinositide biosynthetic enzyme and likely ubiquitination substrate. 2. TLP6 lowers PI4Kβ2 protein abundance and increases its ubiquitinated high-MW forms in a proteasome-dependent manner. 3. PI4Kβ2 complements pi4kβ1,2, confirming functional relevance of the tagged transgene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced PI4Kβ function is associated with dwarfism, short roots, root hair defects, and autoimmunity; overexpression did not show strong penalties in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning PI4Kβ2 stability may modulate immunity, but excessive depletion causes major developmental defects; engineering should avoid full loss of function.
